← All good news Animals

Researchers Used AI to Decode Whale Calls — Then They Started Answering Back

After months of listening, the team played a phrase into the deep. A humpback paused, circled the boat, and replied.

via Ocean Bright · 5 hours ago

🐋

After months of feeding thousands of hours of recordings into a model, researchers began to spot patterns in the clicks and calls — recurring "phrases" that seemed to carry meaning.

When they played one back into the water, a humpback paused, circled the research boat, and answered. The team is careful not to overclaim, but they agree it felt unmistakably like the start of a conversation.

Curated by Cloudbreak — we link straight to the original source as soon as it's available.

How Cloudbreak works

We comb publishers across the web for the stories that make your day a little brighter, show you the headline and a quick summary, then send you straight to the original source for the full story. We don't republish anyone's work — good news belongs to everyone, and credit belongs to the people who reported it.

Spotted something good?

A neighbor, a rescue, a small win on your street — if it made you smile, send it our way.
